title: Traffic-release and undercut-range proxies from same-lap field context
summary: Official F1 strategy explainers emphasize that undercuts depend on being within striking range and on where a car will rejoin after the stop.
rationale: Official F1 strategy explainers emphasize that undercuts depend on being within striking range and on where a car will rejoin after the stop. The current best solutions already model tyre pressure well, but they appear lighter on inferred release-into-traffic context.
implementation_hint: Within each race lap, engineer relative features: position percentile, lap-time delta rank, local neighborhood stats for cars in positions `p-2..p+2`, count of nearby rivals with similar tyre age, and field-relative pace residuals by compound. Derive proxies for `undercut_available`, `overcut_viable`, and `traffic_penalty_if_pit_now`.
expected_effect: Adds orthogonal competitive-state information, especially for midfield battles where stop timing depends more on traffic and track position than on raw tyre age.
risk: Without true gap-to-car-ahead/behind telemetry, these are indirect proxies and may add noise on sparse or unusual laps.
